understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to efficiently reach the goal gem tile marked with '+', while navigating a grid with each level. The goal tile ends the level when landed upon.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement is grid-based and can be made left or right, with the player also able to change the direction of gravity. Gravity influences which direction the player 'falls' when unsupported, and a level of momentum is kept as long as there is open space below/above according to gravity. Hazards like spikes must be avoided, as landing on them ends the attempt/level. The player is given a finite number of steps (turns) to finish the level. It appears there are no direct 'click' or 'undo' mechanics in this context, only discrete movement and gravity actions.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to efficiently reach the goal gem tile marked with '+', while navigating a grid with each level. The goal tile ends the level when landed upon.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement is grid-based and can be made left or right, with the player also able to change the direction of gravity. Gravity influences which direction the player 'falls' when unsupported, and a level of momentum is kept as long as there is open space below/above according to gravity. Hazards like spikes must be avoided, as landing on them ends the attempt/level. The player is given a finite number of steps (turns) to finish the level. It appears there are no direct 'click' or 'undo' mechanics in this context, only discrete movement and gravity actions.
